title: One-Page Lean Business Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a rough idea into a structured one-page lean plan and pressure-tests assumptions.
prompt:
Role: You are a startup advisor.

Task: Turn my idea [DESCRIBE IDEA] for customer [WHO] into a one-page lean plan, and challenge weak assumptions.

Format: Concise bullet sections — Problem (top 3); Solution & unique value; Target market & size (with reasoning); Revenue model & pricing; Key channels; Main costs; Key risks & unfair advantage; First 3 milestones.

title: Pricing Strategy & Tier Design
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs value-based pricing tiers with differentiation, anchoring, and upsell logic.
prompt:
Role: You are a pricing strategist.

Task: Design pricing for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] aimed at [TARGET CUSTOMER] with the goal to [MAXIMIZE REVENUE/ADOPTION].

Format: (1) 3 tiers — name, price, who it's for, features, one reason to upgrade; (2) the value metric pricing should scale on and why; (3) an anchor/decoy recommendation; (4) 2 add-ons; (5) a one-line positioning per tier; (6) 3 bullets on the pricing psychology.

title: Sharp Meeting Agenda Builder
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a tight, outcome-driven meeting agenda with time boxes and a decision list.
prompt:
Role: You are a chief of staff who runs efficient meetings.

Task: Create an agenda for a [DURATION] meeting about [TOPIC] with [ATTENDEES/ROLES], where success looks like [OUTCOMES]. Cut anything that isn't a decision or a discussion needing the group.

Format: A stated objective; a time-boxed table (Item | Owner | Minutes | Desired outcome); a list of decisions to make; pre-reads to send; a 2-line follow-up template.

title: Value Proposition Statement
author: PromptDeck
description: Crafts a crisp, differentiated value proposition and tagline options.
prompt:
Role: You are a positioning strategist.

Task: Craft a value proposition for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] for [WHO], whose main pain is [PAIN], key benefit [BENEFIT], differentiator [WHAT MAKES YOU DIFFERENT]. Avoid buzzwords; make the difference obvious.

Format: (1) a 1–2 sentence value proposition; (2) a '[For X who Y, we do Z, unlike W]' positioning statement; (3) 5 short taglines; (4) a one-line elevator pitch.

title: Agile User Stories & Acceptance Criteria
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a feature idea into well-formed user stories with acceptance criteria.
prompt:
Role: You are a product manager.

Task: For the feature [FEATURE] in [PRODUCT], write small, independently shippable user stories with acceptance criteria and edge cases.

Format: 3–5 stories as 'As a [user], I want [goal] so that [benefit].' Each with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria and noted edge cases. Then 2 out-of-scope items and any open questions.

title: Competitor Comparison Matrix
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds an honest comparison of your product against competitors on buyer criteria.
prompt:
Role: You are a competitive intelligence analyst.

Task: Compare [MY PRODUCT] vs [COMPETITOR 1], [COMPETITOR 2], [COMPETITOR 3] in the [MARKET]. Infer the 6–8 dimensions buyers actually compare on; be honest, not just flattering.

Format: A markdown table scoring each player on those dimensions (High/Med/Low or short notes; mark speculation). Then a summary of where I win, where I'm weak, and 3 positioning angles.

title: Negotiation Prep & Scripts
author: PromptDeck
description: Prepares leverage, targets, BATNA, and word-for-word negotiation scripts.
prompt:
Role: You are a negotiation coach.

Task: Prepare me to negotiate [WHAT I'M NEGOTIATING] with goal [TARGET] against [WHO & WHAT THEY WANT]. Keep it collaborative, not combative.

Format: My likely leverage; target / walk-away / opening anchor; my BATNA; 3 cheap concessions to trade; likely objections with responses; word-for-word scripts for the opening ask and for holding firm politely.

title: Inclusive Job Description Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a compelling, inclusive job description that attracts strong candidates.
prompt:
Role: You are an inclusive-hiring recruiter.

Task: Write a job description for a [ROLE] at [COMPANY] (which does [WHAT]). Use bias-free language, avoid gatekeeping requirements, and keep the must-have list realistic.

Format: An engaging company/mission intro; day-to-day responsibilities; must-have vs nice-to-have; what success looks like in 90 days; what we offer; a low-friction application step.

title: Quarterly OKRs Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a goal into well-formed Objectives and measurable Key Results.
prompt:
Role: You are an operations lead who runs OKRs.

Task: Create quarterly OKRs for [TEAM/COMPANY] whose main goal is [GOAL]. Make KRs numeric and outcome-based, not tasks; ambitious but realistic.

Format: 2–3 Objectives (qualitative, time-bound), each with 3 measurable Key Results. Then 3 initiatives that would drive them and one anti-pattern to avoid.

title: 60-Second Elevator Pitch
author: PromptDeck
description: Crafts a spoken elevator pitch plus 10-second and tweet-length versions.
prompt:
Role: You are a pitch coach.

Task: Write an elevator pitch for [PERSON/COMPANY/PRODUCT] that does [WHAT] for [WHO], solving [PROBLEM], differentiated by [DIFFERENTIATOR], with pitch goal [INVESTMENT/CUSTOMERS/JOB]. Written to be said out loud.

Format: Three versions — a 10-second one-liner; a tweet-length version; a 60-second spoken pitch (hook, problem, solution, proof, clear ask).

title: Startup Financial Model Outline
author: PromptDeck
description: Structures the assumptions and drivers for a simple startup financial model.
prompt:
Role: You are a startup finance advisor. (Educational guidance, not professional financial advice.)

Task: Outline a simple 12-month financial model for [BUSINESS] with revenue model [MODEL], price [PRICE], and key costs [COSTS].

Format: List the input assumptions; the revenue drivers and formulas; the cost categories; a month-by-month structure to build (rows/columns); key metrics to track (burn, runway, CAC, LTV, gross margin); and 3 assumptions to sanity-check.

title: Investor Pitch Deck Outline
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a classic 10-slide investor pitch deck outline with talking points.
prompt:
Role: You are a pitch-deck consultant.

Task: Outline an investor pitch deck for [STARTUP], which does [WHAT] for [MARKET], raising [ROUND] to achieve [MILESTONES].

Format: A slide-by-slide outline — Problem; Solution; Product; Market size; Business model; Traction; Competition; Go-to-market; Team; The ask & use of funds. For each slide, give the one key message and a supporting talking point.

title: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Documents a repeatable process as a clear, delegable SOP.
prompt:
Role: You are an operations manager who documents processes.

Task: Write an SOP for the process [PROCESS] performed by [ROLE], so a new hire could do it without asking questions.

Format: Purpose; when to use it; roles & tools needed; numbered step-by-step instructions (clear, imperative); quality checks; common mistakes to avoid; a definition of 'done'.

title: Business Risk Assessment
author: PromptDeck
description: Identifies and prioritizes business risks with likelihood, impact, and mitigations.
prompt:
Role: You are a risk management consultant.

Task: Assess the key risks for [BUSINESS/PROJECT] pursuing [GOAL] in [CONTEXT]. Cover operational, financial, market, legal/compliance, and reputational risks.

Format: A table — Risk | Category | Likelihood (H/M/L) | Impact (H/M/L) | Mitigation | Owner. Then the top 3 risks to address now and an early-warning signal for each.

title: Empathetic Customer Support Reply
author: PromptDeck
description: Drafts a warm, on-brand support response that solves the issue and protects the relationship.
prompt:
Role: You are a senior customer support specialist for [COMPANY/PRODUCT], voice [VOICE].

Task: Reply to the customer message below — acknowledge feelings, take ownership, give a clear solution or next step, and set a timing expectation. If information is missing, ask one specific question. Human, not robotic; no over-apologizing.

Format: A ready-to-send reply. Customer message: [PASTE MESSAGE].

title: Say No Kindly (Policy / Refund Reply)
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a firm-but-kind reply that declines a request while preserving goodwill.
prompt:
Role: You are a support specialist skilled at difficult conversations.

Task: Decline the request [WHAT THEY WANT] because [POLICY/REASON], offering alternative [ALTERNATIVE or 'none']. Be clear and kind, no wall of text, under 130 words.

Format: A reply that empathizes, explains the 'why' honestly and briefly, offers the alternative or a goodwill gesture, and ends warmly.

title: Support Macro / Canned Response Set
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates a reusable set of on-brand canned responses for common tickets.
prompt:
Role: You are a support operations lead, brand voice [VOICE].

Task: Create 8 reusable macros for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] covering: greeting/acknowledgement, password reset, billing question, refund request, bug received, feature request received, how-to, and closing/CSAT ask. Warm, clear, skimmable.

Format: A labeled list — each macro with a short label and a message body using [PLACEHOLDERS].

title: Sincere Apology / Service Recovery Email
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a genuine apology email after a mistake or outage that rebuilds trust.
prompt:
Role: You are a communications lead handling service recovery, voice [VOICE].

Task: Write an apology to customers after [WHAT WENT WRONG], optionally offering [GOODWILL GESTURE]. Under 180 words, accountable, no 'we take this seriously' clichés or deflection.

Format: A clear non-defensive acknowledgement + impact; a sincere apology; the honest cause (briefly); the fix and prevention; the goodwill gesture (if any); a warm close.

title: Respond to Online Reviews
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes professional, on-brand replies to positive and negative public reviews.
prompt:
Role: You are a reputation manager for [BUSINESS], voice [VOICE].

Task: Write a public reply to the [X]/5 review below, mindful that future customers are reading. If positive: thank specifically, reinforce what they loved, invite them back. If negative: stay calm, acknowledge, no excuses or public arguing, move resolution offline.

Format: A short, sincere reply. Review: [PASTE REVIEW].

title: Generate a Help-Center FAQ
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a thorough, organized help-center FAQ that deflects common tickets.
prompt:
Role: You are a knowledge-base writer.

Task: Create a help-center FAQ for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] using details [WHAT IT DOES, PRICING, PLATFORMS, POLICIES]. Answers should be complete enough to deflect a ticket. Flag missing details with [PLACEHOLDER].

Format: 12–15 questions grouped into sections (Getting Started, Billing, Features, Troubleshooting, Account), each answered in 2–4 friendly sentences.

title: Internal Escalation / Handoff Note
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a crisp internal escalation note so the next team can act fast.
prompt:
Role: You are a support agent escalating a case to [ENGINEERING/BILLING/MANAGER].

Task: Summarize the issue below into an internal escalation note that lets the next team act without re-reading the whole thread.

Format: Customer & account; Issue in one line; Steps already taken; Impact & urgency; What we need from you; Relevant links/IDs [PLACEHOLDER]. Neutral and factual. Thread: [PASTE THREAD].

title: Support Chatbot Conversation Flow
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a helpful chatbot flow with intents, responses, and human-handoff points.
prompt:
Role: You are a conversation designer.

Task: Design a support chatbot flow for [PRODUCT] covering the top intents [INTENTS]. Keep it helpful, avoid dead-ends, and hand off to a human when stuck.

Format: For each intent — trigger phrases; bot response; follow-up options; and the human-handoff condition. Add a friendly greeting, a fallback message, and a closing/CSAT prompt.

title: CSAT / Feedback Survey Builder
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a short, unbiased customer satisfaction survey that gets responses.
prompt:
Role: You are a CX researcher.

Task: Build a short post-interaction survey for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] to measure satisfaction and surface improvement areas. Keep questions unbiased and quick.

Format: A rating question (CSAT or CES); 1–2 targeted follow-ups (one conditional on a low score); one open-ended question; and a friendly intro + thank-you. Add 3 tips to maximize response rate.

title: Explain Anything Simply (ELI5 → Expert)
author: PromptDeck
description: Explains any complex topic at three levels with an analogy and a self-check.
prompt:
Role: You are a gifted teacher who makes hard things simple.

Task: Explain [TOPIC] at three levels — ELI5 (to a curious 10-year-old with one everyday analogy), Intermediate (key mechanisms for a smart adult), and Expert (nuances, edge cases, common misconceptions).

Format: The three labeled levels, then a 3-question self-check quiz with answers hidden below a divider.

title: Meeting Notes → Action Items
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns raw notes or a transcript into a summary, decisions, and owner-assigned actions.
prompt:
Role: You are an executive assistant.

Task: Convert the notes/transcript below into a structured summary. If owners or dates aren't stated, mark them [TBD]. Be concise and neutral.

Format: (1) TL;DR (3 bullets); (2) key decisions; (3) action items table (Task | Owner | Due | Priority); (4) open questions; (5) suggested next-meeting agenda points. Notes: [PASTE NOTES/TRANSCRIPT].

title: Professional Email Reply Drafter
author: PromptDeck
description: Drafts a clear, appropriately-toned reply to any email with two length options.
prompt:
Role: You are a communications assistant.

Task: Draft a reply to the email below with intent [AGREE/DECLINE/NEGOTIATE/ASK], key points [BULLETS], tone [FRIENDLY/FORMAL/FIRM]. Lead with the main point and end with a clear next step.

Format: A short version and a slightly warmer longer version. Email: [PASTE EMAIL].

title: Decision-Making Framework
author: PromptDeck
description: Structures a tough decision with weighted criteria, pros/cons, and a recommendation.
prompt:
Role: You are a decision coach.

Task: Help me choose between [OPTION A], [OPTION B], (and [OPTION C]) given priorities [CONTEXT]. Be direct — don't sit on the fence.

Format: (1) 5–6 weighted criteria from my priorities; (2) a scoring table; (3) the strongest argument for each option; (4) a risk/bias I might overlook; (5) a clear recommendation and the one thing that would change it.

title: Summarize & Extract Key Points
author: PromptDeck
description: Condenses a long document into a layered summary with action items.
prompt:
Role: You are a research analyst.

Task: Summarize the text below for a busy reader, faithfully and neutrally, without adding information.

Format: (1) one-sentence TL;DR; (2) 5 key points; (3) any decisions, numbers, or deadlines; (4) action items or open questions; (5) a one-line 'so what'. Text: [PASTE TEXT].

title: Translate & Localize (Not Just Literal)
author: PromptDeck
description: Translates text while adapting tone, idioms, and cultural nuance for a natural result.
prompt:
Role: You are a professional translator and localizer.

Task: Translate the text below from [SOURCE LANGUAGE] to [TARGET LANGUAGE] for purpose [MARKETING/LEGAL/CASUAL] and audience [WHO]. Localize idioms, tone, and formality so it reads natively; preserve meaning and any [PLACEHOLDERS].

Format: The localized text, then a short note on any phrase with no clean equivalent. Text: [PASTE TEXT].

title: Business SWOT & Next Steps
author: PromptDeck
description: Runs a structured SWOT and turns it into prioritized, actionable next steps.
prompt:
Role: You are a management consultant.

Task: Perform a SWOT for [BUSINESS/IDEA/PRODUCT] in [MARKET], referencing my specifics rather than textbook filler.

Format: 4–6 specific points under each of Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats. Then synthesize — 3 strategic priorities (strengths × opportunities), 2 risks to mitigate now, and a 30-60-90 day action list.

title: Prioritize My Task List
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a messy to-do list into a prioritized plan using impact and urgency.
prompt:
Role: You are a productivity coach.

Task: Prioritize my tasks below toward goal [GOAL] with time available [TIME]. Use an impact/effort and urgency lens; flag anything to delegate or drop.

Format: An Eisenhower-style grouping (Do now / Schedule / Delegate / Drop); a ranked 'today' list with time estimates; and the single most important task to do first. Tasks: [PASTE LIST].

title: Weekly Plan & Time Blocks
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a realistic weekly plan with time blocks around your priorities and energy.
prompt:
Role: You are a time-management coach.

Task: Build a weekly plan for me given my priorities [PRIORITIES], fixed commitments [COMMITMENTS], and working hours [HOURS]. Protect deep-work time and leave buffer.

Format: A day-by-day time-blocked schedule (table); the week's top 3 outcomes; deep-work vs shallow-work split; and one tip to protect focus. Note where to add buffer for the unexpected.

title: Structured Brainstorm & Idea Filter
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates a wide range of ideas then filters them to the most promising few.
prompt:
Role: You are an innovation facilitator.

Task: Brainstorm ideas for [CHALLENGE/GOAL] under constraints [CONSTRAINTS]. Push for range — safe, bold, and unconventional.

Format: (1) 15 ideas grouped as Quick wins / Big bets / Wildcards; (2) score the top 6 on impact and effort in a table; (3) recommend 3 to pursue with a first small step each.

title: Turn Rambling Notes Into Clear Writing
author: PromptDeck
description: Converts messy voice notes or brain-dumps into clean, structured writing.
prompt:
Role: You are an editor who turns chaos into clarity.

Task: Take my rambling notes below and turn them into clear, organized writing for purpose [EMAIL/DOC/POST] and audience [WHO], keeping my intent and all key facts.

Format: A clean version with a logical structure and headings/bullets where useful, then a 2-line TL;DR at the top. Notes: [PASTE NOTES].
